mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in FEDFUNDS reflect aggressive monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically supporting equity valuations. As the Fed cuts rates in response to the 2008 crisis, forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days should be positively biased relative to periods of stable rates.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher in easing episodes than in stable-rate episodes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payroll losses (PAYEMS month-over-month decline) exceed a severe threshold, it signals an accelerating labor market collapse that raises recession probability and drives risk-off equity selling over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2008 payroll losses accelerating into recession
regime_hint: labor market contraction phase of recession
failure_condition: When triggered, SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained collapse in housing starts (HOUST) below prior cyclical lows indicates a structural demand shock centered on residential investment and household balance sheets, which propagates to broader consumer and credit conditions and depresses equity returns over a full quarter horizon during a housing-led recession.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1420k units, near multi-decade lows as of snapshot
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative relationship with HOUST level or trend during housing bust periods (rank IC not distinguishable from zero at 95% confidence).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is rising sharply while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously falling, it signals that monetary policy is reactively chasing a deteriorating labor market rather than leading it, indicating policy is behind the curve. This joint condition amplifies near-term market fear and produces negative SPX returns over a short 5-day horizon before any stabilizing effect of easing manifests.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 2 months while FEDFUNDS falls more than 0.5pp over same period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.4pp asof snapshot may reflect stale/lagged reporting during rapid deterioration
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint-trigger episodes are not significantly more negative than returns following FEDFUNDS declines alone (difference-in-means p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects contracting real economic output and corporate earnings capacity, creating persistent downward pressure on equity prices over short horizons as markets continuously reprice growth expectations lower.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and 5d forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth turns negative or falls sharply toward zero, it signals emerging deflationary pressure that raises real debt burdens and compresses corporate pricing power, causing markets to price in a harder recession and depressing equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL value 302.918 with commodity and oil prices collapsing into late 2008
regime_hint: deflation risk regime during demand collapse
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns following negative CPI MoM prints are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or occur less than 55% negative frequency.

mechanism_narrative: During an active recession regime, month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) directly signal contracting consumer demand, which represents roughly two-thirds of GDP; this weakness feeds through to corporate revenue expectations and produces negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days, an effect that is muted or absent outside recessionary regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS declining sharply in second half of 2008 amid credit crisis
regime_hint: active NBER recession with consumer retrenchment
failure_condition: Conditional on recession regime, correlation between RSAFS monthly change and subsequent 20d SPX return is not significantly positive (Pearson r not > 0 at 95% confidence), or effect disappears outside recession but hypothesis claims regime dependence not observed.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) combined with a simultaneous rise in UNRATE indicates a self-reinforcing demand-labor feedback loop characteristic of deep recessions, where falling output cuts jobs which further cuts spending. This joint deterioration signals a prolonged downturn and predicts negative SPX returns over a 60-day horizon beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is negative while UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 value 20235.878 reported with typical lag amid contraction beginning Q3 2008
regime_hint: deep recession with demand-labor feedback loop
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes do not produce 60d forward returns significantly more negative than episodes triggered by GDPC1 contraction alone (difference-in-means p > 0.05), indicating no incremental interaction effect.
